name: gwosc

metadata.name: gwosc
metadata.summary: A python interface to the GW Open Science data archive

documentation:
# `gwosc` client API

The `gwosc` package provides an interface to querying the open data
releases hosted on <https://gwosc.org> from the GEO, LIGO,
and Virgo gravitational-wave observatories.

## Release status

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/gwosc.svg)](http://badge.fury.io/py/gwosc)
[![Conda version](https://img.shields.io/conda/vn/conda-forge/gwosc.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/gwosc/)  
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/DOI/10.5281/zenodo.1196306.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.1196306)
[![License](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/gwosc.svg)](https://choosealicense.com/licenses/mit/)
![Supported Python versions](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/gwosc.svg)

## Development status

[![Build status](https://git.ligo.org/gwosc/client/badges/main/pipeline.svg)](https://git.ligo.org/gwosc/client/-/pipelines)
![Code coverage](https://git.ligo.org/gwosc/client/badges/main/coverage.svg)
[![Documentation](https://readthedocs.org/projects/gwosc/badge/?version=latest)](https://gwosc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

## Installation

To install:

    conda install -c conda-forge gwosc

or

    pip install gwosc

## Searching for datasets

To search for available datasets (correct as of March 14 2018):

```python
>>> from gwosc import datasets
>>> datasets.find_datasets()
['GW150914', 'GW151226', 'GW170104', 'GW170608', 'GW170814', 'GW170817', 'LVT151012', 'O1', 'S5', 'S6']
>>> datasets.find_datasets(detector='V1')
['GW170814', 'GW170817']
>>> datasets.find_datasets(type='run')
['O1', 'S5', 'S6']
```

To query for the GPS time of an event dataset (or vice-versa):

```python
>>> datasets.event_gps('GW170817')
1187008882.43
>>> datasets.event_at_gps(1187008882)
'GW170817'
```

Similar queries are available for observing run datasets:

```python
>>> datasets.run_segment('O1')
(1126051217, 1137254417)
>>> datasets.run_at_gps(1135136350)  # event_gps('GW151226')
'O1'
```

## Locating data URLs by event name

You can search for remote data URLS based on the event name:

```python
>>> from gwosc.locate import get_event_urls
>>> get_event_urls('GW150914')
['https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/H-H1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/H-H1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126257415-4096.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126257415-4096.hdf5']
```

You can down-select the URLs using keyword arguments:

```python
>>> get_event_urls('GW150914', detector='L1', duration=32)
['https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5']
```

## Locating data URLs by GPS interval

You can search for remote data URLs based on the GPS time interval as
follows:

```python
>>> from gwosc.locate import get_urls
>>> get_urls('L1', 968650000, 968660000)
['https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968646656-4096.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968650752-4096.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968654848-4096.hdf5', 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968658944-4096.hdf5']
```

This arguments for this function are as follows

-   `detector` : the prefix of the relevant gravitational-wave
    interferometer, either `'H1'` for LIGO-Hanford, or `'L1'` for LIGO
    Livingston,
-   `start`: the GPS start time of the interval of interest
-   `end`: the GPS end time of the interval of interest

By default, this method will return the paths to HDF5 files for the 4
kHz sample-rate data, these can be specified as keyword arguments. For
full information, run

```python
>>> help(get_urls)
```

## Query for Timeline segments

You can also search for Timeline segments, based on a flag name, and a
GPS time interval as follows:

```python
>>> from gwosc.timeline import get_segments
>>> get_segments('H1_DATA', 1126051217, 1126151217)
[(1126073529, 1126114861), (1126121462, 1126123267), (1126123553, 1126126832), (1126139205, 1126139266), (1126149058, 1126151217)]
```

The output is a `list` of `(start, end)` 2-tuples which each represent a
semi-open time interval.

For documentation on what flags are available, for example for the O1
science run, see [the O1 data release page](https://gwosc.org/O1/)
(*Data Quality*).

functions.name: gwosc.utils.url_segment
documentation:
Return the GPS segment covered by a URL following T050017

# Parameters

filename

: the path name of a file

# Returns

segment

: the `[start, stop)` GPS interval covered by the given URL

# Notes

[LIGO-T050017](https://dcc.ligo.org/LIGO-T050017/public) declares
a filenaming convention that includes documenting the GPS start integer
and integer duration of a file, see that document for more details.

functions.name: gwosc.utils.url_overlaps_segment
documentation:
Returns True if a URL overlaps a \[start, stop) GPS interval

# Parameters

url

: the URL of a file

segment

: the `[start, stop)` GPS interval to check against

# Returns

overlap?

: `True` if the GPS interval for the URL matches that given,
  otherwise `False`

functions.name: gwosc.utils.urllist_extent
documentation:
Returns the GPS `[start, end)` interval covered by a list or URLs

Parameters
----------
urls : `iterable` of `str`
    the list of URLs

Returns
-------
a, b : 2-`tuple` of int`
    the GPS extent of the URL list

functions.name: gwosc.utils.strain_extent
documentation: Returns the GPS `[start, end)` interval covered by a strain meta dict

functions.name: gwosc.utils.full_coverage
documentation:
Returns True if the list of URLS completely covers a GPS interval

The URL list is presumed to be contiguous, so this just checks that
the first URL (by GPS timestamp) and the last URL can form a segment
that overlaps that given.

functions.name: gwosc.utils.segments_overlap
documentation: Returns True if GPS segment `a` overlaps GPS segment `b`

functions.name: gwosc.tests.test_locate.test_get_urls_version
documentation: Regression test against version not being respected in get_urls

modules.name: gwosc
documentation: GWOSC: a python interface to the GW Open Science data archive

modules.name: gwosc.timeline
documentation:
`gwosc.timeline` provides functions to find segments for a given dataset.

You can search for Timeline segments, based on a flag name, and a
GPS time interval as follows:

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.timeline import get_segments
>>> get_segments('H1_DATA', 1126051217, 1126151217)
[(1126073529, 1126114861), (1126121462, 1126123267), (1126123553, 1126126832), (1126139205, 1126139266), (1126149058, 1126151217)]
```

The output is a `list` of `[start, end)` 2-tuples which each
represent a semi-open time interval.

For documentation on what flags are available, for example for the O1
science run, see [the O1 data release
page](https://gwosc.org/O1/) (*Data Quality*).

functions.name: gwosc.timeline.get_segments
documentation:
Return the \[start, end) GPS segments for this flag

# Parameters

flag

: name of flag, e.g. `'H1_DATA'`

start

: the GPS start time of your query

end

: the GPS end time of your query

host

: the URL of the remote GWOSC server

# Returns

segments

: a list of `[a, b)` GPS segments

functions.name: gwosc.timeline.timeline_url
documentation: Returns the Timeline JSON URL for a flag name and GPS interval

modules.name: gwosc._version
documentation: don't change, don't track in version control

modules.name: gwosc.locate
documentation:
`gwosc.locate` provides functions to determine the file URLs containing
data for a specific dataset.

You can search for remote data URLS based on the event name:

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.locate import get_event_urls
>>> get_event_urls('GW150914')
['https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/H-H1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/H-H1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126257415-4096.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126257415-4096.hdf5']
```

You can down-select the URLs using keyword arguments:

```pycon
>>> get_event_urls('GW150914', detector='L1', duration=32)
['https://gwosc.org/eventapi/json/GWTC-1-confident/GW150914/v3/L-L1_GWOSC_4KHZ_R1-1126259447-32.hdf5']
```

You can search for remote data URLs based on the GPS time interval as
follows:

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.locate import get_urls
>>> get_urls('L1', 968650000, 968660000)
['https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968646656-4096.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968650752-4096.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968654848-4096.hdf5',
 'https://gwosc.org/archive/data/S6/967835648/L-L1_LOSC_4_V1-968658944-4096.hdf5']
```

By default, this method will return the paths to HDF5 files for the 4 kHz
sample-rate data, these can be specified as keyword arguments.
For full information, see {func}`get_urls`.

functions.name: gwosc.locate.get_urls
documentation:
Fetch the URLs from GWOSC regarding a given GPS interval

# Parameters

detector

: the prefix of the relevant GW detector

start

: the GPS start time of your query

end

: the GPS end time of your query

dataset

: the name of the dataset to query, e.g. `'GW150914'`

version

: the data-release version for the selected datasets

sample_rate

: the sampling rate (Hz) of files you want to find

format

: the file format (extension) you want to find

host

: the URL of the remote GWOSC server

# Returns

urls

: the list of remote file URLs that contain data matching the
  relevant parameters

functions.name: gwosc.locate.get_run_urls
documentation:
Fetch the URLs from GWOSC regarding a given event

# Parameters

event

: the ID of the event

detector

: the detector for files you want to find

start

: the GPS start time of your query

end

: the GPS end time of your query

format

: the file format (extension) you want to find

sample_rate

: the sampling rate (Hz) of files you want to find

host

: the URL of the remote GWOSC server

version

: the data-release version for the selected datasets

# Returns

urls

: the list of remote file URLs that contain data matching the
  relevant parameters

functions.name: gwosc.locate.get_event_urls
documentation:
Fetch the URLs from GWOSC regarding a given event

# Parameters

event

: the ID of the event

detector

: the detector for files you want to find

format

: the file format (extension) you want to find

sample_rate

: the sampling rate (Hz) of files you want to find

host

: the URL of the remote GWOSC server

start

: the GPS start time of your query

end

: the GPS end time of your query

version

: the data-release version for the selected datasets

# Returns

urls

: the list of remote file URLs that contain data matching the
  relevant parameters

functions.name: gwosc.urls.sieve
documentation:
Sieve a list of GWOSC URL metadata dicts based on key, value pairs

# Parameters

urllist

: the `'strain'` metadata list, as retrieved from the GWOSC
  server

segment

: a `[start, stop)` GPS segment against which to check overlap
  for each URL

match

: other keywords match **exactly** against the corresponding key
  in the `dict` for each URL

# Yields

dict :

: each URL dict that matches the parameters is yielded, in the same
  order as the input `urllist`

functions.name: gwosc.urls._match_url
documentation:
Match a URL against requested parameters

Returns
-------
None
    if the URL doesn't match the request

tag, version : `str`, `int`
    if the URL matches the request

Raises
------
StopIteration
    if the start time of the URL is _after_ the end time of the
    request

functions.name: gwosc.urls.match
documentation:
Match GWOSC URLs for a given \[start, end) interval

# Parameters

urls

: List of URL paths

start

: GPS start time of match interval

end

: GPS end time of match interval

tag

: URL tag to match, e.g. `'CLN'`

version

: Data release version to match, defaults to highest available
  version

# Returns

urls

: A sub-list of the input, based on matching, if no URLs are matched,
  the return will be empty `[]`.

modules.name: gwosc.api
documentation:
{mod}`gwosc.api` provides the low-level interface functions
that handle direct requests to the GWOSC host.

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_json
documentation:
Fetch JSON data from a remote URL

# Parameters

url

: the remote URL to fetch

kwargs

: other keyword arguments are passed directly to {func}`requests.get`

# Returns

data

: the data fetched from `url` as parsed by
  {meth}`requests.Response.json`

# See also

json.loads

: for details of the JSON parsing

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_dataset_json
documentation:
Returns the JSON metadata for all datasets matching the GPS interval

Parameters
----------
gpsstart : `int`
    the GPS start of the desired interval

gpsend : `int`
    the GPS end of the desired interval

host : `str`, optional
    the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
    https://gwosc.org

Returns
-------
data : `dict` or `list`
    the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returned by `json.loads`

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_run_json
documentation:
Returns the JSON metadata for the given science run parameters

# Parameters

run

: the name of the science run, e.g. `'O1'`

detector

: the prefix of the GW detector, e.g. `'L1'`

gpsstart

: the GPS start of the desired interval

gpsend

: the GPS end of the desired interval

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returned by `json.loads`

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_cataloglist_json
documentation:
Returns the JSON metadata for the catalogue list.

# Parameters

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returned by
  {meth}`requests.Response.json`

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_catalog_json
documentation:
"Returns the JSON metadata for the given catalogue

# Parameters

catalog

: the name of the event catalog, e.g. `GWTC-1-confident`

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returnend by
  {meth}`requests.Response.json`

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_allevents_json
documentation:
"Returns the JSON metadata for the allevents API

# Parameters

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returned by
  {meth}`requests.Response.json`

functions.name: gwosc.api._fetch_allevents_event_json
documentation:
Returns the JSON metadata from the allevents view for a specific event

The raw JSON data are packaged to look the same as if they came from
a full event API query, i.e. nested under `'events`'.

functions.name: gwosc.api._select_to_query
documentation: Parse select string and translate into URL GET parameters

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_filtered_events_json
documentation:
"Return the JSON metadata for the events constrained by select

# Parameters

select

: a list of range constrains for the events.
  All ranges should have inclusive ends (\<= and >= operators).

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returnend by
  {meth}`requests.Response.json`

# Example

```pycon
>>> fetch_filtered_events_json(
...     select=[
...         "mass-1-source <= 5",
...         "mass-2-source =< 10",
...         "10 <= luminosity-distance <= 100",
...     ]
... )
```

functions.name: gwosc.api.fetch_event_json
documentation:
Returns the JSON metadata for the given event.

By default, this function queries across all catalogs and all data-release
versions, returning the highest available version, unless the
`version` and/or `catalog` keywords are specified.

# Parameters

event

: the name of the event to query

catalog

: name of catalogue that hosts this event

version

: restrict query to a given data-release version

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

data

: the JSON data retrieved from GWOSC and returned by `json.loads`

modules.name: gwosc.datasets
documentation:
`gwosc.datasets` includes functions to query for available datasets.

To search for all available datasets:

```pycon
>>> from gwosc import datasets
>>> datasets.find_datasets()
['GW150914', 'GW151226', 'GW170104', 'GW170608', 'GW170814', 'GW170817', 'LVT151012', 'O1', 'S5', 'S6']
>>> datasets.find_datasets(detector='V1')
['GW170814', 'GW170817']
>>> datasets.find_datasets(type='run')
['O1', 'S5', 'S6']
```

To query for the GPS time of an event dataset (or vice-versa):

```pycon
>>> datasets.event_gps('GW170817')
1187008882.43
>>> datasets.event_at_gps(1187008882)
'GW170817'
```

Similar queries are available for observing run datasets:

```pycon
>>> datasets.run_segment('O1')
(1126051217, 1137254417)
>>> datasets.run_at_gps(1135136350)  # event_gps('GW151226')
'O1'
```

To run an event query filtered by merger parameters:

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import query_events
>>> query_events(select=["mass-1-source <= 3.0"])
['GW170817-v3', 'GW190425-v1', 'GW190425-v2', 'GW190425_081805-v3']
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets._match_dataset
documentation: Returns `True` if the dataset metadata matches the target

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.find_datasets
documentation:
Find datasets available on the given GW open science host

# Parameters

detector

: prefix of GW detector

type

: type of datasets to restrict, one of `'run'`, `'event'`, or
  `'catalog'`

segment

: a GPS `[start, stop)` interval to restrict matches to;
  datasets will match if they overlap at any point; this is
  not used to filter catalogs

match

: regular expression string against which to match datasets

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

datasets

: the names of all matched datasets, possibly empty

# Examples

(Correct as of 2018-03-14)

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import find_datasets
>>> find_datasets()
['GW150914', 'GW151226', 'GW170104', 'GW170608', 'GW170814', 'GW170817',
 'LVT151012', 'O1', 'S5', 'S6']
>>> find_datasets(detector='V1')
['GW170814', 'GW170817']
>>> find_datasets(type='event')
['GW150914', 'GW151226', 'GW170104', 'GW170608', 'GW170814', 'GW170817',
 'LVT151012']
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.event_gps
documentation:
Returns the GPS time of an open-data event

# Parameters

event

: the name of the event to query

catalog

: name of catalogue that hosts this event

version

: the version of the data release to use,
  defaults to the highest available version

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

gps

: the GPS time of this event

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import event_gps
>>> event_gps('GW170817')
1187008882.43
>>> event_gps('GW123456')
ValueError: no event dataset found for 'GW123456'
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.event_segment
documentation:
Returns the GPS `[start, stop)` interval covered by an event dataset

# Parameters

event

: the name of the event

detector

: prefix of GW detector

catalog

: name of catalogue that hosts this event

version

: the version of the data release to use,
  defaults to the highest available version

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

start, end

: the GPS `[start, end)` interval covered by this run dataset

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import event_segment
>>> event_segment("GW150914")
segment(1126257415, 1126261511)
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.event_at_gps
documentation:
Returns the name of the open-data event matching the GPS time

This function will return the first event for which
`|eventgps - gps| < = tol`.

# Parameters

gps

: The GPS time to locate

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

tol

: the search window (in seconds), default: `1`

# Returns

event

: the name of the matched event

# Raises

ValueError

: if no events are found matching the GPS time

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import event_at_gps
>>> event_at_gps(1187008882)
'GW170817'
>>> event_at_gps(1187008882, tol=.1)
ValueError: no event found within 0.1 seconds of 1187008882
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.event_detectors
documentation:
Returns the `set` of detectors that observed an event

# Parameters

event

: the name of the event to query

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

version

: the version of the data release to use,
  defaults to the highest available version

# Returns

detectors

: the set of detectors for which data file URLs are included in
  the data release

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import event_detectors
>>> event_detectors("GW150914")
{'H1', 'L1'}
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.run_segment
documentation:
Returns the GPS `[start, stop)` interval covered by a run dataset

# Parameters

run

: the name of the run dataset to query

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

start, end

: the GPS `[start, end)` interval covered by this run dataset

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import run_segment
>>> run_segment('O1')
segment(1126051217, 1137254417)
>>> run_segment('Oh dear')
ValueError: no run dataset found for 'Oh dear'
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.run_at_gps
documentation:
Returns the name of the open-data run dataset matching the GPS time

This function will return the first event for which
`start <= gps < end`

# Parameters

gps

: The GPS time to locate

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query, defaults to
  <https://gwosc.org>

# Returns

run

: the name of the matched observing run

# Raises

ValueError

: if no datasets are found matching the GPS time

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import run_at_gps
>>> run_at_gps(1135136350)
'O1'
>>> run_at_gps(0)
ValueError: no run dataset found containing GPS 0
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.dataset_type
documentation:
Returns the type of the named dataset

# Parameters

dataset

: The name of the dataset to match

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query

# Returns

type

: the type of the dataset, one of 'run', 'event', or 'catalog'

# Raises

ValueError

: if this dataset cannot be matched

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import dataset_type
>>> dataset_type('O1')
'run'
```

functions.name: gwosc.datasets.query_events
documentation:
Return a list of events filtered by the parameters in `select`

# Parameters

select

: A list of strings where each element is a range constrain on the
  event parameters.
  All ranges should have inclusive ends (\<= and => operators).

host

: the URL of the GWOSC host to query

# Examples

```pycon
>>> from gwosc.datasets import query_events
>>> query_events(
...     select=[
...         "mass-1-source >= 1.4",
...         "200 >= luminosity-distance >= 100",
...     ]
... )
['GW190425-v1', 'GW190425-v2', 'GW190425_081805-v3']
```

# Notes

Operators:

- `<=` (or `=<`)
- `=>` (or `>=`)

Parameters:

- gps-time\`,

- `mass-1-source`,

- `mass-2-source`,

- `network-matched-filter-snr`,

- `luminosity-distance`,

- `chi-eff`,

- `total-mass-source`,

- `chirp-mass`,

- `chirp-mass-source`,

- `redshift`,

- `far`,

- `p-astro`,

- `final-mass-source`

For a full description of all parameters see
<https://www.gwosc.org/apidocs/#event5>
